daily_brief: Tensions between the United States and Iran dominate the day's headlines as negotiations resume in Oman following this week's Strait of Hormuz clashes. President Trump issued stark warnings of military action should Iran attempt assassination, while Tehran insisted it had honored ceasefire terms [BBC, Al Jazeera, France 24, The Hindu]. In Spain, emergency crews continue battling one of the country's deadliest wildfires near Almería, with at least 12 dead—including four believed to be British nationals—and 23 still missing amid 40°C temperatures [BBC, The Guardian]. Typhoon Bavi, spanning roughly 1,000km, has forced mass evacuations across Taiwan after killing at least 15 in Philippine landslides, with the storm now bearing down on southeastern China [BBC, France 24]. The EU escalated regulatory pressure on Meta, warning of substantial fines over 'addictive' design features on Facebook and Instagram, while the company separately reversed its controversial AI image-generation tool following global privacy backlash [BBC, Al Jazeera, RNZ]. Meanwhile, developing nations face mounting fiscal strain as UNESCO data reveals 113 countries spent more on foreign debt repayment than education in 2025, with sub-Saharan Africa allocating 3.6 times more to debt servicing [The Guardian].

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Tensions Escalate as Oman Talks Resume Amid Strait of Hormuz Standoff
bullets: US officials demanded Iran publicly declare the Strait of Hormuz open and pledge to stop firing on commercial ships; Iran privately acknowledged the attacks were a 'mistake' by rogue hardliners, according to US media citing unnamed officials
President Trump warned the US would 'completely decimate' Iran if it attempted to assassinate him, stating 1,000 missiles were 'locked and loaded'

VP JD Vance, Secretary of State Marco Rubio, envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner are leading Saturday negotiations in Oman; Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi maintains Iran 'kept its word' on the ceasefire
An explosion was reported in eastern Tehran province Saturday, though the cause remained unclear

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized diplomatic negotiations and both sides' stated positions

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Framed as questioning whether US and Iran are returning to war

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed liveblog coverage focusing on Trump's threats and regional implications

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted funeral context and Trump's inflammatory religious language in threats

top_stories.headline: Deadly Spanish Wildfire Kills at Least 12, Including Four Britons, as 23 Remain Missing
bullets: At least 12 people died in wildfires near Los Gallardos in Almería, Andalusia; four victims found in a burnt-out car with right-hand steering wheel believed to be British nationals
Authorities attribute the fire's cause to a fallen power line, though electricity companies deny responsibility; local officials warn death toll could rise
Sustained heatwave with temperatures around 40°C has fueled wildfires across Southern Europe, particularly in France, Portugal, and Spain
Belgian Foreign Minister confirmed consular services were attempting to contact Belgian nationals who have second homes in the affected region

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Led with human interest angle featuring survivor testimony

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Focused on official statements and diplomatic notifications

top_stories.headline: Typhoon Bavi Forces Mass Evacuations in Taiwan, Kills 15 in Philippines
bullets: Typhoon Bavi, spanning approximately 1,000km—roughly the width of France—triggered landslides killing at least 15 in the Philippines' Mindanao island
More than 14,000 people evacuated in Taiwan as authorities warned of up to 1 meter of potential rainfall; 29,000 soldiers placed on standby
Storm knocked out power to thousands in Japan's Okinawa islands before bearing down on southeastern China, where landfall expected early Sunday
Bavi is the largest typhoon by size to approach Taiwan since 1987, according to the island's Central Weather Administration

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized regional scope and preparedness measures

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on evacuations and ground-level impact in Taiwan

top_stories.headline: EU Warns Meta of Heavy Fines Over 'Addictive' Facebook and Instagram Design
bullets: European Commission issued preliminary findings that infinite scroll, autoplaying videos, and personalized recommendations on Facebook and Instagram encourage 'compulsive use,' particularly among minors
Meta faces potential fines of up to 6% of total global annual turnover if it fails to make suitable changes
Commission specifically criticized Reels and Stories features, arguing they contribute to excessive use and that Meta's existing safeguards are inadequate
Meta spokesperson stated the company 'disagreed with the findings' and cited 'significant steps' taken to protect teens

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Balanced presentation of EU position and Meta's response

top_stories.headline: Meta Reverses AI Image Feature After Global Privacy Backlash
bullets: Meta removed its 'Muse Image' AI feature that allowed users to generate images of people by mentioning their public Instagram accounts
Feature was automatically enabled for millions of users by default; only private accounts and those under 18 were excluded
New Zealand and international experts had criticized the feature as 'entirely unethical,' arguing public content sharing does not constitute consent for AI transformation
Meta acknowledged it 'missed the mark' and withdrew the feature following coverage by BBC, RNZ, Wired, New York Times and widespread negative feedback

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Detailed expert criticism and ethical concerns from academic sources

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Brief video coverage emphasizing swift global backlash
